extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won a sprint finish at Chateauroux to extend his Tour stage win record pursuit, maintaining the green jersey.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET Greg's breakaway expenditure on flat stage before climbing stage severely questioned by commentators given tomorrow's better suit.
MATTHEWS Michael identified as primary green jersey threat via intermediate sprint points on Category climbs near finishes where CAVENDISH drops.
UAE team benefits tactically from POGAČAR sitting 8 seconds back, avoiding yellow jersey's lead-out duties while remaining GC threat.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: pressure mounting to stay calm through remaining sprints as record approaches, historically prone to crashes when overreaching
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: burned energy in 90-mile breakaway day before stage tailored to his abilities, tactically questionable move
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: positioned as strongest green jersey alternative through ability to contest intermediate climbs with sub-20km to finish
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: 8-second deficit provides tactical advantage of avoiding yellow jersey team lead-out burden while remaining GC threat
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: neutral: named by Alpecin directeur as sprint option but team noncommittal on sprint leadership hierarchy
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: listed among sprint candidates with previous Turkey tour win over MERLIER establishing internal hierarchy
